product_depth.justification: All 9 new sections across both files contain actionable, self-contained templates. SA: bounded context checklist, 7 context mapping patterns, entity/VO table, aggregate rules, ubiquitous language glossary, OpenAPI 3.1 skeleton (234 lines), AsyncAPI 3.0 skeleton, versioning comparison, deprecation policy, breaking change classification, STRIDE template with all 6 categories, 5x5 risk matrix, resource estimation table, scaling thresholds, horizontal/vertical comparison, load projection. Ops: reconciliation loop with FluxCD/ArgoCD comparison, GitOps repo layout, drift detection workflow, SLO/SLI template, error budget formula with 3 worked examples, burn-rate alerting, budget policy, toil tracking, complete runbook with 7 sections, P0-P4 severity classification, 5-phase incident lifecycle, post-incident review template, all 4 DORA metrics with classification thresholds and improvement guidance. Exceeds anchor 5.
product_depth.drift_check: Same as prior round. Both rounds added equivalent depth of actionable templates to domain skill files.

functionality.justification: All 24 contract verification checks pass. Both files are additions-only (632 + 422 insertions, 0 deletions). Existing Sections 1-10 preserved in both files. Section numbering sequential. All 4 F-009 steps and all 5 F-010 steps satisfied.
functionality.drift_check: Same as prior round. All checks pass, no regressions.

visual_design.justification: Formatting consistent with existing Sections 1-10: ## Section N headings, --- horizontal rules, ### subsections, #### sub-subsections, pipe-delimited tables, code blocks with language annotations. Some tables are wide but match existing style. No formatting inconsistencies.
visual_design.drift_check: Same as prior round. Same formatting pattern, same minor inherent width constraint on complex tables.

code_quality.justification: All framework references factually accurate: DDD 7 context mapping patterns correct per Evans/Vernon, STRIDE 6 categories correct per Microsoft, OpenAPI 3.1.0 current, AsyncAPI 3.0.0 current, DORA 4 metrics match Accelerate research thresholds, SRE error budget formula correct per Google SRE Book, burn-rate alerting thresholds match Google SRE Workbook, error budget arithmetic verified (43.2/21.6/4.32 min). No broken references, no heading hierarchy issues, no Markdown lint warnings.
code_quality.drift_check: Same as prior round. Both rounds required factual accuracy of domain-specific frameworks; both verified correct.

Each Ops section contains at least one usable template: Section 11 (flow + comparison table + repo layout + procedure), Section 12 (SLO/SLI table + formula + alerting table + policy table + toil table), Section 13 (7-section runbook template), Section 14 (severity table + lifecycle table + review template), Section 15 (4 metric tables + dashboard template)

All 12 F-009 checks pass: Strategic Design heading, 7 context mapping patterns, Tactical Design heading, entity/VO table, Ubiquitous Language Glossary, OpenAPI 3.1 skeleton, AsyncAPI 3.0 skeleton, versioning comparison, STRIDE with 6 categories, risk scoring matrix, Capacity Modeling heading, scaling thresholds table

All 12 F-010 checks pass: Reconciliation Loop heading, GitOps directory structure, Drift Detection heading, SLO/SLI template, error budget formula and burn-rate thresholds, Runbook Template heading, runbook contains Trigger/Impact/Steps/Escalation/Rollback, Severity Classification with P0-P4, post-incident review template, DORA Metrics heading, all four metrics covered, each with Elite/High/Medium/Low classification
git diff HEAD~1 --stat shows 632 insertions 0 deletions for harness-sa/SKILL.md and 422 insertions 0 deletions for harness-ops/SKILL.md. Additions only, existing content preserved.

DORA 4 metric definitions and thresholds match Accelerate research. SRE error budget formula correct per Google SRE Book. codex_detection.detection_method: Three checks: (1) enabledPlugins contains codex@openai-codex, (2) extraKnownMarketplaces contains openai-codex, (3) which codex exits 0
codex_detection.detection_result: Codex detected via all three methods. Not used because changes are documentation-only Markdown -- adversarial code review not applicable.
codex_detection.fallback_reason: Not a fallback. Codex review not applicable for Markdown-only documentation changes.

internal_consistency.justification: Both files follow conventions from Sections 1-10. Sequential section numbering, consistent heading hierarchy, matching table styles, horizontal rule separators. Terminology consistent within each file.

intentionality.justification: Builder report documents specific customization decisions: team dynamic column in DDD patterns, invariant boundaries in aggregate rules, pagination/error schemas in OpenAPI, correlation_id in AsyncAPI headers, affected-component column in STRIDE, Google SRE Workbook burn-rate windows, stateless/stateful differentiation in drift detection.

craft.justification: Consistent structure: heading levels sequential, horizontal rules between sections, tables with header rows and separators, code blocks with language annotations. No broken formatting or orphan references.

fitness_for_purpose.justification: Every section contains actionable templates usable without external references. OpenAPI/AsyncAPI are copy-paste-ready. STRIDE/capacity tables have placeholder values. Runbook is complete for on-call use. DORA includes measurement methods and data sources. SRE includes verified arithmetic examples.
